channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: ARC Raiders PvE Map Condition ‘Security Agreement’ Revealed by Playtests
item.content:encoded:
ARC Raiders is getting a PvE-based map condition called Security Agreement (per a translation), but there’s a huge catch – it’s only coming to Chinese playtests for now. It’s not clear if it’ll land on the core version of the game anytime soon, but if it does, it could be a huge win for PvE-focused fans that brings many of them back to the dwindling extraction adventure title.

On the Chinese website advertising the tests, a news article was posted describing the new map condition, which has been rumored to appear on the localized version of the game for several weeks. You can find out everything you need to know about Security Agreement below.

Will ARC Raiders Get PvE Globally?

The concept of PvE surfacing in ARC Raiders has plagued gamers for the longest time. Or, at least, since the game hit the market in October 2025 and skyrocketed up the charts. Embark Studios, the game’s developer, has had a rocky time of late, and it came to a head when the team announced a ‘slowing down’ of major game updates.

Regardless, the title is still set to surface in China, with a specialized, localized version that includes Chinese text on all in-game assets, and likely some tuned story elements or narrative aspects, as is the region and precedent.

What China is also getting is a PvE-focused map condition called Security Agreement (translated).

Here’s the spiel from the Chinese site that advertised what this condition entails (again, translated):

Thanks to the efforts of the raiders, communication equipment in the area has been temporarily restored. Shani, the security chief of Speranza, has used the restored communications relays to establish a security agreement in the area to prevent raiders from harming each other.

However, some malicious raiders may use overloaded batteries to block communications and bypass the security agreement. After actively disconnecting communications, the antenna on a raider’s backpack will flash red; please be vigiliant.

The ‘Security Agreement’ PvE friendly event for the ARC Raiders Chinese server will be launched during the ARC Light Test. The first closed beta for the Chinese server has already begun.

That’s about it. Those participating in the Chinese ARC Raiders test are actively enjoying a PvE mode right now, and that, of course, doesn’t sit well with the fans around the world who have been begging for a PvE condition of some sort since day one.

As I said, there’s no indication of whether this event will ever reach the Western market, but I can’t see why not. It would be overwhelmingly easy for Embark Studios to add it to the base platform.

Do you want to see more PvE in ARC Raiders? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out what the future of PlayStation ‘might’ look like

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Head of Sony Interactive Believes Gaming’s Future Is Indie
item.content:encoded:
Former head of Sony Interactive Entertainment Shuhei Yoshida comments on the current state of the game industry.

Shuhei Yoshida was the President of SIE Worldwide Studios at Sony Interactive Entertainment from 2008 to 2019 and the head of PlayStation Indies from 2019 until his retirement in 2025. He still believes in the power of Indie Games, as you recently stated in an interview that he no longer plays AAA games because they require too much time.

Indie Developers Will Lead Gaming’s Next Era, Says Ex-Sony Exec

During an interview with GameSpot, Yoshida stated that he believes the rise of digital game storefronts and the ability for any developer to be a publisher has had the biggest impact on the video game industry. He spoke about how it’s easier to create a game than ever before, noting that the number of games being published will continue to increase, but he believes that that will mean the quality will also rise.

“Because of that amount of games being produced,” Yoshida says, “it becomes even harder to get the games noticed.”

When asked how he believes future indie projects can stand out amid the rising number of titles, Yoshida stated that it’s much easier for indie developers to find success once they have created a game that connects with an audience. This makes it easier to have a future community of testers.

“When your games are successful, they will feel that they are part of the success, so they will become even more passionate about supporting you–it’s very, very important to have a strong sense of community support,” he said.

The producer of the Sonic the Hedgehog games recently commented on what AAA game developers can learn from independent studios. Read those comments here.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Sonic Producer Sees Lessons for AAA Studios in Indie Game Scene
item.content:encoded:
The producer of the Sonic the Hedgehog games, Takashi Iizuka, thinks that AAA game developers could learn some things from indie developers in the same way that he believes major movie studios could learn from indie movie successes.

Sonic the Hedgehog is celebrating his 35th anniversary. He’s also been one of the top gaming icons throughout that time. That being said, not every Sonic game is reviewed glowingly. Still, the series always looks to evolve and try new things, such as the current Sonic Pico Park indie game, revealed during Summer Game Fest 2026.

Sonic Producer Takashi Iizuka Compares Indie Game Success to Indie Film Hits

During a recent interview with GamesRadar+, Takashi Iizuka spoke about how Sega approaches collaborating with indie developers on projects such as the aforementioned Sonic Pico Park. He noted that it’s a major investment to make a top game in a franchise, but indie developers can quickly transition from generating an idea to executing that idea.

He says that major game development companies could “learn from the indie development scene.” Also, he finds it to be “really stimulating working with those indie developers, because you get to feel that smaller team energy and that quickness of working to get an idea into an experience.”

He would compare this to the movie industry. Currently, Backrooms and Obsession, two smaller-budget films, are having great success in theaters amid films with crazy high budgets attached to known IPs. Iizuka pointed out the parallels between the two entertainment mediums, noting that everything is defined by what customers actually enjoy.

“You see movies like Backrooms and [Obsession], these much smaller creative efforts that are still becoming these great successful hits, so I do see a parallel in the movie industry to what’s kind of happening in the game industry with the amount of investment and the actual entertainment that people are consuming and enjoying.”

Sonic has recently made a significant impact in the movie industry. The fourth film in the franchise is set to be released in 2027. You can learn more about that here. In other news related to movies based on video games, the recent Super Mario Galaxy movie became the first film in 2026 to gross $1 billion.

Check out everything announced during Summer Games Fest here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: G2 and Team Heretics Are First to Be Eliminated from VALORANT Masters London Playoffs
item.content:encoded:
The first elimination matches of the VALORANT Masters London playoffs bracket have wrapped, and they’ve seen G2 Esports and Team Heretics ejected from the tournament. They’re the first teams to drop out of the playoffs after making it all this way in London, and their dismissal marks a shake-up in viewer expectations.

G2 collapsed with a 0-2 loss against FUT Esports, and Team Heretics fell out of the running after a 1-2 defeat handed down by Leviatán. These two organizations walk away from London steeped in commiserations but around $35,000 richer.

VALORANT Masters London Playoffs Push On

The departure of both G2 and Team Heretics from the VALORANT Masters London tournament comes as a surprise, as both of these squads were top seeds from their respective regions. The stacked field proved to be a little too much, and ultimately, someone has to go first.

Now that G2 and Heretics are gone, all eyes are on whoever comes next.

Paper Rex and Vitality have pushed for a solid start in the opening upper bracket matches, including the former team securing an insane 13-1 win on Ascent against Leviatán. There’s still every potential for Leviatán and FUT Esports to push through the lower bracket and into the grand final, of course – it just depends who else joins them down there from tomorrow.

VALORANT Masters London delivers its final week this week, and when the dust has settled, one team will walk away with $350,000 in their pocket, as well as a hefty eight VCT points.

We could see a redemption story of sorts surface yet, with PRX clawing a first-place victory after falling in second place against Nongshim RedForce in Santiago in March.

Which team do you think will win the VALORANT Masters London event? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out everything you need to know about The International 15 qualifiers

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1100)—Answers for Monday, June 15
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 15)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • HORSE
  • ANEMONE
  • SNAKE
  • CHANGE
  • LEGS
  • DOG
  • LARKSPUR
  • SHOWER
  • DRAGON
  • TRACTION
  • PRIMP
  • MONKSHOOD
  • MOMENTUM
  • PHLOX
  • ACCESSORIZE
  • STAMINA

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Terms linked to running
  • Green – Things you’d do to get ready
  • Blue – Signs of the Chinese zodiac
  • Purple – Rare flower names

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 15!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 15 (#1100)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – LEGS, MOMENTUM, STAMINA, TRACTION
  • Green – ACCESSORIZE, CHANGE, PRIMP, SHOWER
  • Blue – DOG, DRAGON, HORSE, SNAKE
  • Purple – ANEMONE, LARKSPUR, MONKSHOOD, PHLOX

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
